result.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: For each bitstring $S_i$ in the given set, find the shortest prefix $P_i$ such that $P_i$ is not a prefix of any other bitstring $S_j$ (where $j 
eq i$). If $S_i$ is itself a prefix of another bitstring $S_j$, the shortest unique prefix for $S_i$ must be $S_i$ plus one additional bit (though in the context of the problem constraints where we extend existing strings, we ensure $S_i$ is no longer a prefix of $S_j$ by appending a bit that differentiates it, or more simply, the problem asks for the shortest prefix that uniquely identifies the cow. If $S_i$ is a prefix of $S_j$, $S_i$ cannot be uniquely identified by any of its own prefixes; thus, we must extend $S_i$ by one bit to make it unique relative to $S_j$. The set of these shortest unique prefixes is the solution.

JUSTIFICATION: The problem is a classic application of a Trie (prefix tree). By inserting all bitstrings into a Trie, the shortest unique prefix for any string $S_i$ is the path from the root to the first node that has only one descendant belonging to the set of original strings. If a string $S_i$ is a prefix of another string $S_j$, the node representing the end of $S_i$ will have a child, meaning it is not unique; in this case, we must extend $S_i$ by one bit to satisfy the uniqueness condition.
